results.title: ST Engineering divests entire equity interest in US-based construction player LeeBoy for US$290 million - The Business Times
ST Engineering divests entire equity interest in US-based construction manufacturer LeeBoy for US$290 million - The Business Times
results.content: ST Engineering divests entire equity interest in US-based construction manufacturer LeeBoy for US$290 million - The Business Times Image 6: share icon Image 9 Image 10 [SINGAPORE] Singapore Technologies Engineering (ST Engineering) : S63 +1.02% on Tuesday (Jun 24) announced that it will be selling its entire equity interest in ST Engineering LeeBoy, a US-based construction equipment manufacturer, for an estimated US$290 million. results.title: ST Engineering Pursues Growth Despite Tariff Disruptions - Aviation Week Network
results.content: # ST Engineering Pursues Growth Despite Tariff Disruptions With a footprint that spans Singapore, China, Vietnam, the U.S. and Europe, ST Engineering boasts one of the largest MRO networks worldwide, logging more airframe maintenance labor hours than any other provider. Chen Chuanren is the Southeast Asia and China Editor for the Aviation Week Network’s (AWN) Air Transport World (ATW) and the Asia-Pacific Defense Correspondent for AWN, joining the team in 2017. **ST Engineering Pursues Growth Despite Tariff Disruptions** is part of our *Aviation Week & Space Technology* - *Inside MRO* and AWIN subscriptions. **Subscribe now** to read this content, plus receive full coverage of what's next in technology from the experts trusted by the commercial aircraft MRO community.

results.title: VVDN Acquires GGS Engineering, Expanding its ER&D Portfolio in Automotive, MedTech and Aerospace Industries - The Manila Times
results.content: VVDN Acquires GGS Engineering, Expanding its ER&D Portfolio in Automotive, MedTech and Aerospace Industries | The Manila Times  Image 1: The Manila Times VVDN Acquires GGS Engineering, Expanding its ER&D Portfolio in Automotive, MedTech and Aerospace Industries  Image 5: The Manila Times Image 6: The Manila Times VVDN Acquires GGS Engineering, Expanding its ER&D Portfolio in Automotive, MedTech and Aerospace Industries GURUGRAM, India, Aug. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- VVDN Technologies, a global provider of software, product engineering and electronics manufacturing services & solutions, today announced the acquisition of GGS Engineering Services, a leading engineering solutions company specializing in offering enhanced ER&D services for the Automotive, MedTech and Aerospace industries.

results.title: BGF completes exit from BB7 following growth in fire safety sector - International Fire & Safety Journal
results.content: ## BGF completes exit from BB7 following growth in fire safety sector ## Fire safety leader BB7 enters new phase under Certania after BGF exit BGF has announced the completion of its exit from London-based fire safety consultancy BB7. During its partnership with BGF, BB7 expanded into the UK’s largest specialist consultancy in fire and façade engineering. BGF said its funding enabled BB7 to expand its senior leadership team, introduce new systems and adopt a data-led approach to decision making. Ben Bradford, CEO of BB7, said: “With BGF, we set out an ambitious plan to build the UK’s leading fire and façade engineering consultancy. results.title: Medialink Group Launches Japan Subsidiary - Variety
results.content: Variety # Hong Kong-Based IP Management Company Medialink Group Expands Into Japan With New Subsidiary Launch (EXCLUSIVE) Medialink Group Limited has officially launched a new subsidiary in Japan, marking a significant expansion for the Hong Kong-based intellectual property management company. The new Japanese subsidiary will focus on three key areas to expand Medialink’s IP management ecosystem: pursuing merger and acquisition opportunities or strategic investments in IP-related companies in Japan, co-investing in Japanese intellectual properties, and forming strategic partnerships with Japanese IP owners and licensors. results.title: American Airlines CEO Rejects United Merger, Hints At Alaska Deal - Live and Let's Fly
results.content: Home » American Airlines » American Airlines CEO Rejects United Merger, Hints At Alaska Deal. # American Airlines CEO Rejects United Merger, Hints At Alaska Deal. American Airlines CEO Robert Isom reportedly told employees that a merger with United Airlines is off the table, but signaled that other transactions could make sense, including a closer partnership with Alaska Airlines or the acquisition of select Spirit Airlines assets. While Isom ruled out a merger with United, he did not rule out a closer partnership with Alaska:. But his openness to Alaska or selective Spirit assets shows American is at least thinking creatively about how to close the gap with stronger rivals. AA may not be in a position to move right now in the way United (or Delta) is, but we will see how closely the partnership with Alaska Airlines becomes in the weeks and months ahead. * ## Malarkey: United CEO Scott Kirby Claims Buying American Airlines Would Create Jobs, Lower Fares, And Save America.

results.title: Clear Channel Outdoor Holdings, Inc. Agrees to be Acquired by Mubadala Capital, in Partnership with TWG Global, for $6.2 Billion - PR Newswire
results.content: (NYSE: CCO) ("Clear Channel" or the "Company"), a leader in U.S. out-of-home (OOH) advertising, today announced that it has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Mubadala Capital, in partnership with TWG Global ("TWG"). Certain statements in this press release, including statements regarding the proposed acquisition of Clear Channel by the investor group (the "Merger"), common shareholder approvals for the Merger, any expected timetable for completing the Merger, the expected benefits of the Merger and any other statements regarding Clear Channel's future expectations, beliefs, plans, objectives, financial conditions, assumptions or future events or performance that are not historical fact constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. This press release is being made with respect to the proposed Merger and related transactions (collectively, the "proposed transaction") involving Clear Channel and Mubadala Capital.
